rulings.Case Title: ROBERT G BAKER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS RELFECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Aerojet-General Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production regarding asbestos content of materials attributable to it and, even if it had, plaintiff has created a triable issue by his declaration. Defendant¿s first through fifth objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND CRONIN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Hennessey Industries, Inc.¿s motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted. Liability against Hennessey is predicated on the contention that Hennesey¿s brake shoe grinding machines failed to protect plaintiffs¿ decedent from exposure to asbestos fibers released from asbestos-containing brake linings. A product manufacturer has no duty to persons whose injury results from defects in the products of others. (Garman v. Magic Chef, Inc. (1981) 117 Cal.App.3d 634; Taylor v. Elliott Turbomachinery Co., Inc. 171 (2009) Cal.App.4th 564). The complaint does not allege that Hennessey sold an asbestos-containing product or that it had any control over the design and manufacture of the asbestos-containing brake linings used in conjunction with its machines. Tellez-Cordova v. Campbell-Hausfeld/Scott Fetzger Co. (2004) 129 Cal.App.4th 577 is distinguishable because the power tools at issue in that case had no function until they were used with toxic-containing disks. (Id. at 584 (¿[U]se with the specified wheels, discs, and grinders was the inevitable use.¿). Here, there is no allegation that defendant¿s machines could only function in conjunction with asbestos-containing brake linings, or that the machines and linings formed a single defective system over which the defendant exercised significant control. The machines are more analogous to the stove in Garman and the acid in Blackwell v. Phelps Dodge Corp. (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 372. As in these cases, the injury complained of would not have occurred but for the defendant¿s product, but it was a defect in the product of another which proximately caused the injury. The only alleged defect in the defendant¿s machines is that they either caused another defective product to injure the decedent. Under Garman and Blackwell, the defendant has no liability under these facts. Plaintiffs¿ request for leave to amend is denied on the

As in these cases, the injury complained of would not have occurred but for the defendant¿s product, but it was a defect in the product of another which proximately caused the injury. The only alleged defect in the defendant¿s machines is that they either caused another defective product to injure the decedent. Under Garman and Blackwell, the defendant has no liability under these facts. Plaintiff¿s request for leave to amend is denied on the

rulings.Case Title: MICKEY ENDSLEY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Joyce Endsley As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Plaintiff's motion to appoint Joyce Endsley As successor-in-interest And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For Mickey Endsley, Deceased Plaintiff, and for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES NAYLOR JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Underground Construction Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant has satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff has failed to create a triable fact regarding asbestos content of any products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s foundation objection to Mr. Ay¿s declaration is sustained. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MILTON TURNER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 9. Defendant McCord Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant has satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to create a triable fact regarding asbestos content of any McCord gaskets that Mr. Turner may have been exposed. Defendant¿s speculation and inadmissible conclusion objections to Mr. Cohen¿s declaration are sustained. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE VALLADON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 11. Defendant Howard J White, Inc.¿s demurrer to all causes of action alleged against it in the first amended complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The first amended complaint insufficiently alleges the asbestos-containing products for which defendant is sought to be held liable. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK BANDUCCI VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 12. Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.¿ motion to dismiss is granted. No opposition filed. A hearing is required unless defense counsel notifies plaintiff of the tentative ruling and plaintiff states that he does not intend to contest the tentative ruling. =(220/HEK)
